metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the concrete implementation of 'same-location suppression': is it a prompt instruction in plan_review_hedge.md (suppress if location matches an E6 finding), a post-pass1 dedup filter, or a checklist item in the hedge criterion's rubric? Name the code site where dedup will live.
findings.finding: The plan's dedup mechanism — 'same-location suppression' to avoid double-reporting hedge findings already caught by E6.no_hedges — names a mechanism that does not exist in the current pipeline. `mint_finding_id()` hashes `finding-text + criteria` (not location), and there is no location-based dedup anywhere in the pass1→partition_findings flow. The plan states this as a design choice and requires it to be tested, but never specifies how same-location suppression will be implemented.
findings.location: ## What (decisions made) — 'Dedup vs E6.no_hedges = same-location suppression'
findings.suggested_fix: The lowest-blast-radius option is a rubric-level instruction in plan_review_hedge.md: 'If the hedge is in an AC clause, tag it E6.no_hedges and suppress this criterion — do not double-report.' This requires no pipeline changes and is testable with a FakeRunner case.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify concretely where 'same-location suppression' lives in the pipeline (e.g., a post-pass3 filter keyed on `(location, finding_text_hash)`, or a Pass-1 instruction to the hedge finder to ABSTAIN when `location` is an AC line) — the codebase has no existing same-location cross-criterion suppression to reuse.
findings.finding: The plan asserts 'same-location suppression' as the dedup mechanism between the new hedge finder and E6.no_hedges, but no same-location suppression infrastructure exists in the plan-review pipeline. Searches for 'same_location', 'suppress_same_location', and 'suppress_duplicate' return zero hits in the plan-review codebase. This is net-new dedup machinery. The Rule-of-Three test fails: there is no existing pattern of location-keyed cross-criterion suppression in pass1_chunk/verifier/pass3 that could be reused. The plan does not specify where this logic lives, what data structure it uses, or whether it is part of Pass-3 partition_findings or a post-processing step.
findings.location: What (decisions made) / 'Dedup vs E6.no_hedges = same-location suppression, tested by a case where a hedge in an AC (E6 territory) is not double-reported'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify what 'same-location suppression' means operationally (which existing hook/layer implements it, or what new code is required) and compare to at least one established alternative (e.g., merge hedge detection into E6's checklist as a sub-key rather than a separate criterion), justifying the chosen approach.
findings.finding: The 'same-location suppression' dedup between the new hedge finder and E6.no_hedges is a novel cross-criterion suppression pattern with no established precedent in the orchestrator or partition_findings infrastructure. The plan asserts it as a solution but names no existing suppression mechanism, provides no comparison to an established approach (e.g., shared checklist key, criteria merge, post-pass dedup filter), and gives no Rule-of-Three rationale for why this specific mechanism is chosen.
findings.location: What (decisions made) — G-7(b), 'Dedup vs E6.no_hedges = same-location suppression'; AC line 3 'hedge_dedup'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the dedup mechanism precisely: define 'same location' (e.g. same AC line number, same `location` field value), where suppression executes (orchestrator post-processing, verifier prompt instruction, or Pass-3 filter), and which finding is kept when both E6.no_hedges and the hedge finder fire on the same location.
findings.finding: The dedup rule between the hedge finder and E6.no_hedges is stated as 'same-location suppression' but the suppression mechanism and the definition of 'same location' are both unspecified. No existing code implements same-location suppression (confirmed absent by codebase search), making this a protocol referenced but not defined.
findings.location: What (decisions made) — G-7(b): 'Dedup vs E6.no_hedges … = same-location suppression'; AC3

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add the new hedge criterion id to `CANONICAL_LLM` in `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/registry.py` (required for `validate-routing` to pass without ORPHAN error) and include this as an explicit edit target in the plan.
findings.finding: The plan asserts that registering the hedge criterion as exec:1-TURN in criteria_routing.json will allow `validate-routing` to exit 0, but the validate-routing gate (registry.py:429-432) checks BOTH that every CANONICAL_LLM criterion has a routing entry AND that every routing entry has a corresponding CANONICAL_LLM membership (orphan check). A new criterion id added to criteria_routing.json but absent from CANONICAL_LLM (registry.py:84-122) will be flagged as an ORPHAN and cause validate-routing to fail. The plan never mentions updating CANONICAL_LLM.
findings.location: What (edit targets) / AC2: 'Hedge detector registered as exec:1-TURN in criteria_routing.json … Proof: validate-routing exits 0'
findings.suggested_fix: Add `registry.py` (the CANONICAL_LLM frozenset update) to the 'What (edit targets)' section alongside criteria_routing.json and plan_review_hedge.md.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify whether the hedge criterion id belongs in `CANONICAL_LLM` (built-in) or the `project.` overlay namespace, and include the corresponding `CANONICAL_LLM` update (or overlay activation) in the edit-targets list so `validate-routing exits 0` is achievable.
findings.finding: Adding a new criterion id to `criteria_routing.json` without also adding it to `CANONICAL_LLM` in `registry.py` will cause `validate_packaged_routing()` (the `validate-routing` command cited as AC proof) to fail with an orphan-key error. The plan's AC explicitly requires `validate-routing exits 0`, but the plan never mentions updating `CANONICAL_LLM`.
findings.location: ## What (edit targets) / AC line: 'validate-routing exits 0'
findings.suggested_fix: Add the hedge criterion id to `CANONICAL_LLM` in `registry.py` and add a `plan_review_hedge.md` prompt file in `src/rebar/llm/reviewers/` — the same pattern as every other built-in criterion. Alternatively, if project-scoped, document that it routes via the `.rebar/criteria_routing.json` overlay `activate` list and explain why built-in status is not appropriate.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify whether the hedge criterion uses the existing `project.<name>` overlay path (no CANONICAL_LLM change needed) or is added as a built-in to `CANONICAL_LLM` — the choice determines whether `criteria_routing.json` registration alone suffices or whether `registry.py:CANONICAL_LLM` must also be updated to pass the validate-routing CI gate.
findings.finding: The plan registers 'plan_review_hedge.md' as a new exec:1-TURN criterion in criteria_routing.json but never states whether it is added to CANONICAL_LLM (the hardcoded frozenset in registry.py:84) or activated through the existing project-overlay path (project.hedge prefix + activate list). The validate-routing gate (registry.py:429-432) enforces strict CANONICAL_LLM ↔ criteria_routing.json parity: an entry in criteria_routing.json that is NOT in CANONICAL_LLM is flagged as an ORPHAN and fails CI. Conversely, omitting it from criteria_routing.json while adding it to CANONICAL_LLM also fails. The existing machinery for adding a net-new criterion WITHOUT touching CANONICAL_LLM is the project-overlay path (project.<name> prefix + activate), already used and tested. The plan does not explain which path it uses, leaving a load-bearing seam unspecified.
findings.location: What (decisions made) / 'Hedge detector registered as exec:1-TURN in criteria_routing.json with plan_review_hedge.md'
findings.suggested_fix: Prefer `project.hedge` (the existing overlay path): add `"project.hedge": {"exec": "1-TURN", …}` to `.rebar/criteria_routing.json` under `plan_review` and list it in `activate`. This reuses the exact machinery ADR 0015 built and avoids touching the frozen `CANONICAL_LLM` frozenset.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify how the three new test functions (`test_e4_scope_exclusion`, `test_hedge_finder`, `hedge_dedup`) integrate with `test_plan_review_evals.py`'s existing parametrize structure — either as new entries in `PLAN_REVIEW_SPECS` (requiring new eval-spec JSON files, also unenumerated) or as standalone functions — and list any additional files created.
findings.finding: The AC proof commands reference three test function names (`test_e4_scope_exclusion`, `test_hedge_finder`, `hedge_dedup`) in `tests/unit/test_plan_review_evals.py`, but none of these functions exist in that file today. The file's current structure uses a `PLAN_REVIEW_SPECS` parametrize list for existing specs; adding new per-criterion recall cases for E4 and the hedge finder would require either extending that list or adding new standalone test functions. The plan does not enumerate this as a CREATION or describe how the new tests integrate with the existing parametrize structure.
findings.location: AC1: 'author recall case test_e4_scope_exclusion in tests/unit/test_plan_review_evals.py'; AC2: 'recall case test_hedge_finder green'; AC3: 'pytest ... -k hedge_dedup green'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `registry.py` (specifically the `CANONICAL_LLM` frozenset) to the edit targets for the hedge criterion, so that `validate-routing` passes bidirectional parity and the new criterion enters `effective_criteria`.
findings.finding: The plan lists `plan_review_E4.md`, `plan_review_hedge.md`, and `criteria_routing.json` as edit targets but omits `registry.py` where `CANONICAL_LLM` — a hardcoded frozenset — must be updated. The `validate-routing` parity gate (which the ACs cite as proof) enforces bidirectional parity: every `CANONICAL_LLM` member needs a routing entry AND every routing entry must be in `CANONICAL_LLM`. Adding the hedge criterion only to `criteria_routing.json` triggers the 'ORPHAN' error at the parity gate; the integration as described would fail its own proof command.
findings.location: ## What (edit targets) / AC line: 'validate-routing exits 0'
findings.suggested_fix: Add `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/registry.py` to the edit targets section: 'Add the hedge criterion id to the `CANONICAL_LLM` frozenset (registry.py:84-122) alongside the criteria_routing.json entry — the parity gate requires both.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace `pytest ... -k hedge_dedup` in AC3 with the full explicit path (e.g., `pytest tests/unit/test_plan_review_evals.py -k hedge_dedup`) matching the pattern used in AC1 and AC2.
findings.finding: AC3's pytest proving command uses a path placeholder (`...`) rather than a concrete file path, making it non-executable as written.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, AC3: `pytest ... -k hedge_dedup`

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Update the story title to reflect the actual decision (exec:1-TURN hedge detector), e.g. 'WS2: E4 detectors + 1-TURN hedge detector feeding the shared sub-answer', so the headline matches the body's stated decision.
findings.finding: The story title promises an 'exec:DET hedge detector' but the body explicitly implements the hedge detector as exec:1-TURN (and argues against exec:DET). The headline and the body contradict each other on a named technical decision, which will confuse future readers and reviewers who reference the title.
findings.location: Story title 'WS2: E4 detectors + exec:DET hedge detector feeding the shared sub-answer' vs. What (decisions made) section — G-7(b) HEDGE DETECTOR block
findings.suggested_fix: Rename to: 'WS2: E4 detectors + exec:1-TURN hedge detector feeding the shared sub-answer'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Assign a canonical criterion ID for the hedge detector (e.g. `E7` or `G7`) and specify that it must be added to `CANONICAL_LLM` in `registry.py` alongside the `criteria_routing.json` entry, so the parity gate passes.
findings.finding: The hedge criterion has no specified criterion ID and is not listed as an addition to `CANONICAL_LLM` in `registry.py`. The `validate_packaged_routing()` parity gate (used as proof in AC1 and AC2) enforces that every `criteria_routing.json` entry must be in `CANONICAL_LLM` and vice versa — an unregistered criterion makes validate-routing exit non-zero, directly breaking the AC proofs.
findings.location: What (edit targets); AC2: 'validate-routing exits 0'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate `.github/workflows/test.yml` and `.github/workflows/gerrit-verify.yaml` as external consumers of `criteria_routing.json` that the parity gate change must not break, and confirm the edit ordering (create plan_review_hedge.md before or atomically with the criteria_routing.json entry).
findings.finding: The CI parity gate consumers of `criteria_routing.json` — `.github/workflows/test.yml` (line 232) and `.github/workflows/gerrit-verify.yaml` (line 189) — both run `python -m rebar.llm.plan_review.registry validate-routing` and are not enumerated as external consumers requiring no additional changes. Adding a new criterion entry to criteria_routing.json will exercise these gates; they are real external callers outside the plan_review dir that could fail.
findings.location: What (edit targets): 'criteria_routing.json (register the hedge criterion exec:1-TURN + E4 checklist entries)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Capture the exec:DET-exclusion rationale (engine_b.scan operates on ASTs, not plan-prose) in a durable artifact — either a header comment in plan_review_hedge.md, a CLAUDE.md note in the plan_review module, or a lightweight ADR — so future contributors know not to migrate the hedge finder to exec:DET.
findings.finding: The plan's key architectural decision — routing the hedge detector as exec:1-TURN rather than exec:DET, with the rationale that engine_b.scan operates on code-file ASTs and cannot scan plan-prose — is captured only in this ephemeral plan document, not in any durable design artifact (ADR, CLAUDE.md, or a comment in plan_review_hedge.md itself). A future contributor seeing a hedge criterion handled differently from DET invariants may try to 'fix' it back to exec:DET, breaking the pipeline silently.
findings.location: What (decisions made) — G-7(b) HEDGE DETECTOR rationale block
findings.suggested_fix: Add a `## Routing rationale` block at the top of `plan_review_hedge.md`: 'This criterion is registered exec:1-TURN (not exec:DET) because engine_b.scan (det_invariants.py) operates on code-file ASTs/patterns and cannot scan plan-prose. Moving to exec:DET would silently skip all plan-prose hedge detection. See also orchestrator.py:332 (route_criteria strips exec:DET before pass1_chunk).'

findings.finding: Acceptance-criteria / definition-of-done quality issues.
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify whether `committed_work_relies_on_unbacked_claim` is a criterion id (so the hedge finder tags its findings with it) or a new Pass-2 sub-answer field (requiring a verifier contract extension), and describe the concrete data-flow link so the WS1 dependency is mechanically testable.
findings.finding: The plan's claim that the hedge finder 'feeds WS1's committed_work_relies_on_unbacked_claim sub-answer' conflates two different things: a Pass-2 BINARY sub-answer (the fixed vocabulary in `GRADED_BINARY` at `decide.py:31-38`) with a finding-level criterion tag. A 1-TURN finding flowing through pass1->verifier->pass3 produces a finding tagged with a criterion id, not a new named sub-answer. The mechanism by which a hedge finding 'feeds' a specific named sub-answer is never specified and cannot be inferred from the existing architecture.
findings.location: ## Why / ## What (decisions made) — 'feeding WS1's committed_work_relies_on_unbacked_claim sub-answer'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define 'same-location suppression' precisely (e.g., same criterion+location tuple, same AC line number, or same sentence span) so the criteria_routing.json rule and test fixture share a common, unambiguous granularity.
findings.finding: The dedup rule 'same-location suppression' between the hedge finder and E6.no_hedges is not precisely defined — 'same location' could mean same AC line, same sentence, same paragraph, or same file section. An executor implementing the suppression logic in criteria_routing.json cannot determine the granularity without guessing.
findings.location: What (decisions made) — G-7(b) HEDGE DETECTOR, dedup paragraph; AC line 3 (hedge_dedup)
findings.suggested_fix: Add one sentence: 'Same-location = identical (criterion_id, location_string) tuple; if E6.no_hedges and the hedge finder both fire on the same location string, drop the hedge finder finding.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the `abstain-with-coverage` record structure for the E4 scope-exclusion sub-check: specify the field names in the coverage dict (or finding annotation), the `reason` value vocabulary, and how this differs from a standard AGENT-tier pass-not-applicable response.
findings.finding: The `abstain-with-coverage` branch for the E4 scope-exclusion sub-check has no defined outcome vocabulary or record structure. The plan says 'ABSTAIN-with-coverage' and 'Add an AC for the abstain branch' but never specifies what fields the coverage record carries, how it differs from a standard DET abstain (which uses `DetResult` with a `coverage` dict), or where the abstain record surfaces in the output.
findings.location: What (decisions made) — G-4: E4 scope-exclusion sub-check

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Justify why the hedge detector must be a new criterion file (plan_review_hedge.md) rather than an addition to E4's updated SCAN PROTOCOL, given E4's current prompt already names 'hedges' in scope — or explicitly carve the scope boundary (e.g., E4 = codebase-grounded hedges, hedge finder = plan-prose structural hedges) in the plan.
findings.finding: The plan introduces plan_review_hedge.md as a new exec:1-TURN criterion, but the EXISTING plan_review_E4.md prompt body already reads 'hedges/confident-assertions' — meaning hedge detection is nominally already within E4's scope. The plan sharpens E4's confident-assertion side with a lexicon but silently extrudes the hedge side into a separate criterion file without justifying why this is a new criterion rather than a targeted expansion of E4's SCAN PROTOCOL (the same file being edited by this story).
findings.location: What (decisions made) — G-7(b) HEDGE DETECTOR section; edit target 'a new plan_review_hedge.md prompt'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a one-sentence scope-boundary statement to the G-7(b) decision section: 'E4 fires on hedges that substitute for a codebase probe (grounded); the hedge finder fires on plan-prose hedges that substitute for a proof or commitment (structural). These are non-overlapping on the codebase-grounding axis.' This satisfies anti-repackaging by specifying a discriminating dimension.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a lexicon-specific proof to AC1 (e.g., a `test_e4_assertion_lexicon` recall case that checks each enumerated assertion frame is present in plan_review_E4.md's SCAN PROTOCOL, or extend AC1's existing test to cover both deliverables explicitly).
findings.finding: AC1 bundles two distinct deliverables (the confident-assertion lexicon AND the scope-exclusion sub-check) under a single proof that names only the scope-exclusion test ('test_e4_scope_exclusion'). The validate-routing check verifies routing consistency, not lexicon content or completeness. There is no lexicon-specific test or proof artifact, so the AC's verify step does not actually measure the first half of what it claims.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, AC1
findings.suggested_fix: Split AC1 into two sub-bullets: (a) lexicon proof — `pytest … -k e4_assertion_lexicon` green, where the test checks that each enumerated frame (e.g. 'X already does/handles/returns/supports Y', 'X is safe/idempotent/atomic', etc.) appears in plan_review_E4.md; (b) scope-exclusion proof — existing `test_e4_scope_exclusion` green.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC (or extend AC2) with an integration-level proving command that runs a synthetic plan through the full pipeline and asserts a hedge finding appears in pass3 output — not only that registration validates and the unit recall test passes.
findings.finding: No AC verifies the hedge finder's end-to-end routing through the actual pass1_chunk → verifier → pass3 pipeline; only a unit-level recall test (test_hedge_finder) is specified. The plan's core architectural claim — that exec:1-TURN routes correctly through all three stages — is unproven by any integration-level check.
findings.location: AC2 (Hedge detector registered…flows through pass1_chunk->verifier->pass3); What (decisions made) G-7(b)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Acknowledge (in the plan or AC) whether existing E4 tests cover the old 'scan for assertions' behavior and confirm they remain valid or will be updated to reflect the sharpened lexicon.
findings.finding: The plan sharpens the existing E4 'scan for assertions' behavior by adding a concrete lexicon, which is a behavior change to an existing criterion. The plan does not acknowledge whether existing E4-based tests (or E4 findings already in the pass1_chunk pipeline) will produce different results under the new, more enumerated trigger set, nor does it note whether those tests need updating.
findings.location: What (decisions made) — G-7(a) block; AC line 1 (test_e4_scope_exclusion)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly classify `plan_review_hedge.md` as a new-file CREATION in the edit-target list (G1G2 requires CREATION=new behavior to be labeled out-of-scope or in-scope with justification, not left implicit).
findings.finding: The plan names `plan_review_hedge.md` as a new edit target (under 'What (edit targets)') and AC2 proof expects it to exist as a registered prompt, but this file does not exist anywhere in the codebase — it is a pure CREATION. Per G1G2, CREATION=new behavior=out-of-scope classification is required, and the plan does not classify it as such.
findings.location: What (edit targets): 'a new plan_review_hedge.md prompt (the 1-TURN hedge finder)'
findings.suggested_fix: In 'What (edit targets)', annotate: 'a new plan_review_hedge.md prompt (CREATION — new 1-TURN hedge finder criterion; in-scope for this story as the primary deliverable)'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the output schema for `plan_review_hedge.md`: specify the criterion id it emits in `criteria[]`, how it populates `location` (plan section vs AC line), and confirm these conform to the P1Finding contract that pass1_chunk consumes.
findings.finding: The new `plan_review_hedge.md` prompt file has no defined output schema: the plan specifies what the hedge finder should detect but never defines what structured output it must produce (which P1Finding fields are required, what the `criteria` array value should be, how `location` is populated for a prose-level hedge). Every existing 1-TURN criterion prompt relies on this contract to flow through pass1_chunk correctly.
findings.location: What (decisions made) — G-7(b) HEDGE DETECTOR; AC2

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the integration protocol for `committed_work_relies_on_unbacked_claim`: state whether it is a new GRADED_BINARY sub-answer, a checklist key, or a finding annotation field, and describe how the hedge finder and E4 populate it — even as a forward reference to WS1's contract that WS2 must conform to.
findings.finding: The `committed_work_relies_on_unbacked_claim` sub-answer — which both E4 and the hedge finder are said to 'feed' — has no defined vocabulary, field placement, or integration protocol in this plan. The plan defers its definition to WS1 but does not specify how a 1-TURN finder finding maps to this sub-answer, leaving the hedge prompt author unable to know what to emit.
findings.location: Why section; What (decisions made) — G-7(b): 'lets Pass-2 grade the hedge substance via committed_work_relies_on_unbacked_claim'; Provenance section

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
